title: Method and apparatus for coupling an electromagnetic wave to a transmission medium
abstract: Aspects of the subject disclosure may include, a system that can be configured for generating an electromagnetic wave and coupling the electromagnetic wave to a transmission medium with a reduced loss of radiation of the electromagnetic wave into free space. The system can also be configured to receive an electromagnetic waves form the transmission medium with a reduced loss of radiation of the electromagnetic wave into free space. Other embodiments are disclosed.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A device, comprising: a transmitter that facilitates generation of an electromagnetic wave, wherein the transmitter facilitates generation of the electromagnetic wave according to a first communication signal, wherein the first communication signal operates in a first spectral segment, wherein the electromagnetic wave operates in a second spectral segment that differs from the first spectral segment, wherein the first communication signal conforms to a first signaling protocol, and wherein the electromagnetic wave conveys the first communication signal in the second spectral segment without modifying the first signaling protocol; a receiver that facilities receiving another electromagnetic wave; a dielectric material comprising a plurality of tapered endpoints; and a coupler coupled to the transmitter and to the receiver, wherein the dielectric material is disposed on a first portion of a surface of a transmission medium, wherein the coupler is coupled to a portion of the dielectric material, wherein each of the plurality of tapered endpoints has a contact surface that conforms to the first portion of the surface of the transmission medium, wherein each of the plurality of tapered endpoints is tapered in a first direction such that a first height at a first position closer to the coupler is greater than a second height at a second position that is further from the coupler, wherein each of the plurality of tapered endpoints is tapered in a second direction such that a first width at the first position is greater than a second width at the second position, wherein the coupler facilitates guiding the electromagnetic wave to the plurality of tapered endpoints of the dielectric material, wherein the plurality of tapered endpoints facilitates reducing a radiation of the electromagnetic wave as the electromagnetic wave propagates to a second portion of the surface of the transmission medium, 

 wherein the receiver facilitates receiving the another electromagnetic wave that propagates from the second portion of the surface of the transmission medium to the coupler via the plurality of tapered endpoints, wherein the receiver further facilitates obtaining a second communication signal conveyed by the another electromagnetic wave, and wherein the another electromagnetic wave is generated by another device without modifying a second signaling protocol of the second communication signal. 2. The device of claim 1, wherein the transmission medium comprises an uninsulated conductor, an insulated conductor, or a dielectric core. 3. The device of claim 1, wherein the first portion of the surface of the transmission medium and the second portion of the surface of the transmission medium do not overlap. 4. The device of claim 1, wherein at least two of the plurality of tapered endpoints vary in at least one physical dimension. 5. The device of claim 1, wherein the second communication signal operates in a third spectral segment, and wherein the another electromagnetic wave operates in a fourth spectral segment that differs from the second spectral segment. 6. The device of claim 1, wherein the coupler comprises a conductive coupler. 7. The device of claim 1, wherein the first signaling protocol comprises a Long- Term Evolution (LTE) protocol or a 5G protocol. 8. The device of claim 1, wherein the second signaling protocol comprises a Long- Term Evolution (LTE) protocol or a 5G protocol. 9. The device of claim 1, wherein the first communication signal is from a mobile device. 10. The device of claim 1, wherein the second communication signal is from a mobile device. 11. The device of claim 1, wherein the transmitter and the receiver are part of a transceiver. 12. A method, comprising: generating, by a transmitter, an electromagnetic wave, wherein: the electromagnetic wave is generated according to a first communication signal, the first communication signal operates in a first spectral segment, the electromagnetic wave operates in a second spectral segment that differs from the first spectral segment, the first communication signal conforms to a first signaling protocol, and the electromagnetic wave conveys the first communication signal in the second spectral segment without modifying the first signaling protocol, a dielectric material is disposed on a first portion of a surface of a transmission medium, the dielectric material comprises a plurality of tapered endpoints, a coupler is coupled to the transmitter, the coupler is coupled to a portion of the dielectric material, each of the plurality of tapered endpoints has a contact surface that conforms to the first portion of the surface of the transmission medium, each of the plurality of tapered endpoints is tapered in a first direction such that a first height at a first position closer to the coupler is greater than a second height at a second position that is further from the coupler, and each of the plurality of tapered endpoints is tapered in a second direction such that a first width at the first position is greater than a second width at the second position, 

 the coupler facilitates guiding the electromagnetic wave to the plurality of tapered endpoints of the dielectric material, and the plurality of tapered endpoints facilitates reducing a radiation of the electromagnetic wave as the electromagnetic wave propagates to a second portion of the surface of the transmission medium; and receiving, by a receiver, another electromagnetic wave, wherein: the coupler is coupled to the receiver, the receiver receives the another electromagnetic wave that propagates from the second portion of the surface of the transmission medium to the coupler via the plurality of tapered endpoints, the receiver obtains a second communication signal conveyed by the another electromagnetic wave, and the another electromagnetic wave is generated by another device without modifying a second signaling protocol of the second communication signal. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the transmission medium comprises an uninsulated conductor, an insulated conductor, or a dielectric core. 14. The method of claim 12, wherein the first portion of the surface of the transmission medium and the second portion of the surface of the transmission medium do not overlap. 15. The method of claim 12, wherein the first communication signal is from a first mobile device and the second communication signal is from a second mobile device.
